# Nitro

Nitro is a full-stack server framework that extends Vite applications with production-ready server capabilities. It provides filesystem routing, code-splitting, built-in support for storage, caching, and databases—all runtime-agnostic and deployable anywhere. Nitro compiles routes at build time, eliminating the need for a runtime router, making it ideal for serverless hosting with near-0ms boot time regardless of project size.

With Nitro, you can create server and API routes inside a `routes/` directory where each file maps directly to a URL path. It integrates seamlessly with Vite as a plugin, and `vite build` produces an optimized `.output/` folder containing both frontend and backend code ready to deploy on Node.js, Bun, Deno, Cloudflare Workers, Netlify, Vercel, and many more platforms with zero configuration.

## Vite Integration

Nitro integrates as a Vite plugin to add server-side capabilities to any Vite project.

```typescript
// vite.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from "vite";
import { nitro } from "nitro/vite";

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [nitro()],
});
```

## Nitro Configuration

Configure Nitro using `nitro.config.ts` to set the server directory and other options.

```typescript
// nitro.config.ts
import { defineNitroConfig } from "nitro/config";

export default defineNitroConfig({
  serverDir: "./server",
  preset: "cloudflare_pages", // optional: target specific platform
  experimental: {
    database: true,
    tasks: true,
  },
  features: {
    websocket: true,
  },
  runtimeConfig: {
    apiSecret: "default-secret", // override with NITRO_API_SECRET env var
  },
});
```

## Route Handlers with defineHandler

Create API routes using `defineHandler` for type-safe request handling. Files in `routes/` or `api/` directories are automatically mapped to URL paths.

```typescript
// server/api/hello.ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";

export default defineHandler(() => {
  return { message: "Hello Nitro!" };
});

// Accessible at: GET /api/hello
// Response: { "message": "Hello Nitro!" }
```

## Dynamic Route Parameters

Use bracket syntax `[param]` in filenames to capture URL parameters. Parameters are accessible via `event.context.params`.

```typescript
// server/routes/users/[id].ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";

export default defineHandler((event) => {
  const { id } = event.context.params;
  return { userId: id, name: `User ${id}` };
});

// GET /users/123 → { "userId": "123", "name": "User 123" }
```

## HTTP Method-Specific Routes

Append HTTP method to filename to restrict route to specific methods. Supported: `get`, `post`, `put`, `delete`, `patch`, `head`, `options`.

```typescript
// server/routes/users.post.ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";

export default defineHandler(async (event) => {
  const body = await event.req.json();
  // Save user to database
  return { created: true, user: body };
});

// POST /users with JSON body
// curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/users -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"name":"John"}'
```

## Catch-All Routes

Use `[...param]` syntax to capture all remaining URL segments including slashes.

```typescript
// server/routes/docs/[...slug].ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";

export default defineHandler((event) => {
  const { slug } = event.context.params;
  return { path: slug }; // slug contains everything after /docs/
});

// GET /docs/guide/getting-started → { "path": "guide/getting-started" }
```

## Middleware

Middleware files in `middleware/` directory run before route handlers. They can modify the request context but should not return values.

```typescript
// server/middleware/auth.ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";

export default defineHandler((event) => {
  const token = event.req.headers.get("authorization");
  if (token) {
    event.context.user = { authenticated: true, token };
  }
});

// server/routes/protected.ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";

export default defineHandler((event) => {
  if (!event.context.user?.authenticated) {
    return new Response("Unauthorized", { status: 401 });
  }
  return { message: "Protected content", user: event.context.user };
});
```

## Cached Handlers with defineCachedHandler

Cache route responses using `defineCachedHandler`. Supports stale-while-revalidate (SWR) pattern by default.

```typescript
// server/routes/cached-data.ts
import { defineCachedHandler } from "nitro/cache";

export default defineCachedHandler(
  async () => {
    // Expensive operation - cached for 1 hour
    const data = await fetch("https://api.example.com/data").then((r) => r.json());
    return data;
  },
  {
    maxAge: 60 * 60, // Cache for 1 hour
    swr: true, // Serve stale while revalidating
    name: "external-data",
  }
);
```

## Cached Functions with defineCachedFunction

Cache function results that are used across multiple handlers.

```typescript
// server/utils/github.ts
import { defineCachedFunction } from "nitro/cache";

export const getGitHubStars = defineCachedFunction(
  async (repo: string) => {
    const response = await fetch(`https://api.github.com/repos/${repo}`);
    const data = await response.json();
    return data.stargazers_count;
  },
  {
    maxAge: 60 * 60, // 1 hour
    name: "ghStars",
    getKey: (repo: string) => repo,
  }
);

// server/routes/stars/[...repo].ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";
import { getGitHubStars } from "~/utils/github";

export default defineHandler(async (event) => {
  const { repo } = event.context.params;
  const stars = await getGitHubStars(repo);
  return { repo, stars };
});

// GET /stars/nitrojs/nitro → { "repo": "nitrojs/nitro", "stars": 5000 }
```

## KV Storage with useStorage

Access the built-in key-value storage layer. Default is in-memory; configure drivers for persistence.

```typescript
// server/routes/storage-example.ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";
import { useStorage } from "nitro/storage";

export default defineHandler(async () => {
  const storage = useStorage();

  // Set a value
  await storage.setItem("user:1", { name: "John", email: "john@example.com" });

  // Get a value
  const user = await storage.getItem("user:1");

  // Check if exists
  const exists = await storage.hasItem("user:1");

  // Get all keys
  const keys = await storage.getKeys("user");

  // Remove a value
  await storage.removeItem("user:1");

  return { user, exists, keys };
});
```

## Storage Configuration

Configure storage drivers in `nitro.config.ts` for different backends.

```typescript
// nitro.config.ts
import { defineNitroConfig } from "nitro/config";

export default defineNitroConfig({
  storage: {
    redis: {
      driver: "redis",
      url: "redis://localhost:6379",
    },
    fs: {
      driver: "fs",
      base: "./data",
    },
  },
  devStorage: {
    redis: {
      driver: "fs",
      base: "./.data/redis", // Use filesystem in development
    },
  },
});
```

## Database with useDatabase

Built-in SQL database layer using SQLite by default. Requires `experimental.database: true`.

```typescript
// server/routes/db-example.ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";
import { useDatabase } from "nitro/database";

export default defineHandler(async () => {
  const db = useDatabase();

  // Create table
  await db.sql`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    name TEXT,
    email TEXT
  )`;

  // Insert data with parameterized values (SQL injection safe)
  const userId = crypto.randomUUID();
  await db.sql`INSERT INTO users VALUES (${userId}, ${"John Doe"}, ${"john@example.com"})`;

  // Query data
  const { rows } = await db.sql`SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ${userId}`;

  return { users: rows };
});
```

## Database Configuration

Configure multiple database connections with different connectors.

```typescript
// nitro.config.ts
import { defineNitroConfig } from "nitro/config";

export default defineNitroConfig({
  experimental: {
    database: true,
  },
  database: {
    default: {
      connector: "sqlite",
      options: { name: "app-db" },
    },
    analytics: {
      connector: "postgresql",
      options: {
        url: "postgresql://user:pass@host:5432/analytics",
      },
    },
  },
  devDatabase: {
    default: {
      connector: "sqlite",
      options: { name: "dev-db" },
    },
  },
});
```

## Plugins with definePlugin

Extend Nitro's runtime behavior with plugins. Auto-registered from `plugins/` directory.

```typescript
// server/plugins/logger.ts
import { definePlugin } from "nitro";

export default definePlugin((nitroApp) => {
  // Hook into request lifecycle
  nitroApp.hooks.hook("request", (event) => {
    console.log(`[${new Date().toISOString()}] ${event.req.method} ${event.req.url}`);
  });

  // Hook into response lifecycle
  nitroApp.hooks.hook("response", (res, event) => {
    console.log(`[${new Date().toISOString()}] Response: ${res.status}`);
  });

  // Hook into error handling
  nitroApp.hooks.hook("error", (error, { event }) => {
    console.error(`Error on ${event?.req.url}:`, error.message);
  });

  // Hook into server shutdown
  nitroApp.hooks.hook("close", async () => {
    console.log("Server shutting down...");
  });
});
```

## Tasks with defineTask

Define and run background tasks. Requires `experimental.tasks: true`.

```typescript
// server/tasks/db/migrate.ts
export default defineTask({
  meta: {
    name: "db:migrate",
    description: "Run database migrations",
  },
  async run({ payload, context }) {
    console.log("Running migrations...", payload);
    // Perform migration logic
    return { result: "Migration completed" };
  },
});

// server/routes/admin/migrate.ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";
import { runTask } from "nitro/task";

export default defineHandler(async (event) => {
  const { result } = await runTask("db:migrate", {
    payload: { version: "1.0.0" },
  });
  return { success: true, result };
});

// Run via CLI: nitro task run db:migrate --payload '{"version":"1.0.0"}'
// Or via API: GET /_nitro/tasks/db:migrate
```

## Scheduled Tasks

Configure tasks to run on a cron schedule.

```typescript
// nitro.config.ts
import { defineNitroConfig } from "nitro/config";

export default defineNitroConfig({
  experimental: {
    tasks: true,
  },
  scheduledTasks: {
    "* * * * *": ["cache:cleanup"], // Every minute
    "0 * * * *": "reports:generate", // Every hour
    "0 0 * * *": ["db:backup", "logs:rotate"], // Daily at midnight
  },
});
```

## WebSocket Handler

Enable real-time communication with WebSocket support. Requires `features.websocket: true`.

```typescript
// server/routes/_ws.ts
import { defineWebSocketHandler } from "nitro";

export default defineWebSocketHandler({
  open(peer) {
    peer.send({ type: "welcome", message: `Hello ${peer}!` });
    peer.subscribe("chat");
    peer.publish("chat", { type: "join", user: peer.toString() });
  },

  message(peer, message) {
    const text = message.text();
    if (text === "ping") {
      peer.send({ type: "pong" });
    } else {
      peer.publish("chat", {
        type: "message",
        user: peer.toString(),
        text: text,
      });
    }
  },

  close(peer) {
    peer.publish("chat", { type: "leave", user: peer.toString() });
  },
});

// Client connection: ws://localhost:3000/_ws
```

## Route Rules Configuration

Configure caching, redirects, proxying, CORS, and authentication at the route level.

```typescript
// nitro.config.ts
import { defineNitroConfig } from "nitro/config";

export default defineNitroConfig({
  routeRules: {
    // Caching with SWR
    "/blog/**": { swr: 600 }, // 10 minutes

    // Full cache options
    "/api/data/**": {
      cache: { maxAge: 60, swr: true },
    },

    // Redirects
    "/old-page": { redirect: "/new-page" },
    "/legacy/**": { redirect: { to: "/v2/**", status: 301 } },

    // Proxy
    "/proxy/api/**": { proxy: "https://api.example.com/**" },

    // CORS
    "/api/public/**": {
      cors: true,
      headers: { "access-control-allow-methods": "GET,POST" },
    },

    // Basic authentication
    "/admin/**": {
      basicAuth: { username: "admin", password: "secret" },
    },

    // Disable for sub-path
    "/admin/public/**": { basicAuth: false },

    // Custom headers
    "/assets/**": {
      headers: { "cache-control": "public, max-age=31536000, immutable" },
    },

    // Prerendering
    "/about": { prerender: true },
  },
});
```

## HTML Renderer Configuration

Configure HTML templates for SPA or SSR with dynamic template processing.

```typescript
// nitro.config.ts
import { defineNitroConfig } from "nitro/config";

export default defineNitroConfig({
  renderer: {
    template: "./index.html", // HTML template file
    static: false, // Enable dynamic template processing
  },
});
```

```html
<!-- index.html with dynamic template syntax -->
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <title>{{ $URL.pathname }} - My App</title>
  </head>
  <body>
    <script server>
      const data = await fetch("https://api.example.com/config").then((r) => r.json());
    </script>
    <div id="app">
      <h1>Welcome {{ $COOKIES["user"] || "Guest" }}</h1>
      <pre>{{ JSON.stringify(data) }}</pre>
    </div>
    <? if ($METHOD === 'POST') { ?>
    <p>Form submitted!</p>
    <? } ?>
  </body>
</html>
```

## Custom Renderer Handler

Create a fully custom renderer for SSR or custom HTML generation.

```typescript
// server/renderer.ts
export default function renderer({ req }: { req: Request }) {
  const url = new URL(req.url);

  return new Response(
    `<!DOCTYPE html>
    <html>
    <head><title>SSR App</title></head>
    <body>
      <h1>Server-rendered page</h1>
      <p>Path: ${url.pathname}</p>
      <p>Time: ${new Date().toISOString()}</p>
    </body>
    </html>`,
    { headers: { "content-type": "text/html; charset=utf-8" } }
  );
}
```

```typescript
// nitro.config.ts
import { defineNitroConfig } from "nitro/config";

export default defineNitroConfig({
  renderer: {
    handler: "./server/renderer.ts",
  },
});
```

## Server Assets

Bundle and access static files at runtime via storage layer.

```typescript
// nitro.config.ts
import { defineNitroConfig } from "nitro/config";

export default defineNitroConfig({
  serverAssets: [
    {
      baseName: "templates",
      dir: "./templates",
    },
  ],
});
```

```typescript
// server/routes/email.ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";
import { useStorage } from "nitro/storage";

export default defineHandler(async () => {
  const templates = useStorage("assets/templates");

  // Get template content
  const html = await templates.getItem("welcome.html");

  // Get metadata
  const meta = await templates.getMeta("welcome.html");

  // List all templates
  const keys = await templates.getKeys();

  return { html, meta, availableTemplates: keys };
});
```

## OpenAPI Documentation

Auto-generate OpenAPI documentation for your API routes.

```typescript
// nitro.config.ts
import { defineNitroConfig } from "nitro/config";

export default defineNitroConfig({
  experimental: {
    openAPI: true,
  },
  openAPI: {
    meta: {
      title: "My API",
      description: "API documentation",
      version: "1.0.0",
    },
    ui: {
      scalar: { theme: "purple" },
    },
  },
});
```

```typescript
// server/routes/api/users.ts
import { defineRouteMeta, defineHandler } from "nitro";

defineRouteMeta({
  openAPI: {
    tags: ["users"],
    description: "Get all users",
    parameters: [{ in: "query", name: "limit", required: false }],
  },
});

export default defineHandler(() => {
  return [{ id: 1, name: "John" }];
});

// Access docs at: /_scalar, /_swagger, /_openapi.json
```

## Deployment Presets

Build for specific deployment targets using presets. Auto-detected in CI/CD environments.

```bash
# Build for specific platform
nitro build --preset cloudflare_pages
nitro build --preset vercel
nitro build --preset netlify

# Or set via environment variable
NITRO_PRESET=cloudflare_pages nitro build
```

```typescript
// nitro.config.ts for platform-specific options
import { defineNitroConfig } from "nitro/config";

export default defineNitroConfig({
  preset: "vercel",
  vercel: {
    config: { runtime: "nodejs20.x" },
  },
  // Or for Cloudflare
  // preset: "cloudflare_pages",
  // cloudflare: {
  //   wrangler: { compatibility_date: "2025-01-01" },
  // },
});
```

## Error Handling

Define custom error handlers for production error responses.

```typescript
// server/error.ts
export default defineNitroErrorHandler((error, event) => {
  const isDev = process.env.NODE_ENV === "development";

  return new Response(
    JSON.stringify({
      error: error.message,
      statusCode: error.statusCode || 500,
      stack: isDev ? error.stack : undefined,
    }),
    {
      status: error.statusCode || 500,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    }
  );
});
```

```typescript
// nitro.config.ts
import { defineNitroConfig } from "nitro/config";

export default defineNitroConfig({
  errorHandler: "~/error",
});
```

## Summary

Nitro is designed for building full-stack JavaScript/TypeScript applications that deploy anywhere. Its primary use cases include creating API backends with filesystem-based routing, adding server-side rendering to Vite applications, building serverless functions with automatic code-splitting, and implementing real-time features with WebSocket support. The built-in storage and caching layers enable persistent data handling and performance optimization without external dependencies.

Integration patterns typically involve using Nitro as a Vite plugin for unified frontend/backend development, leveraging route rules for declarative caching and proxying, utilizing the plugin system for cross-cutting concerns like logging and authentication, and configuring deployment presets for zero-configuration deployments to platforms like Cloudflare, Vercel, Netlify, AWS, and traditional Node.js servers. The framework's runtime-agnostic design means the same codebase can target different deployment environments by simply changing the build preset.
